title: Yosys Parsing Error in RTL Design File (rtl.v) During Synthesis
body:
### Version

 Yosys 0.47+86

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

I am encountering a parsing error while using Yosys to synthesize my RTL design file rtl.v. Below is the description of the problem and the steps I followed:
read_verilog rtl.v
synth
write_verilog syn_yosys.v

When running the above commands, Yosys fails to parse the rtl.v file and reports an error. I have attached a screenshot of the error message for reference.
![yosys_question](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b194785c-d69c-4ac0-99a8-432aaff2648e)

Steps Taken:

I used the command read_verilog rtl.v to load the Verilog RTL file.
The synth command was executed to perform synthesis.
The write_verilog syn_yosys.v command was issued to output the synthesized Verilog file.
However, the parsing error occurs during the read_verilog step, preventing the synthesis process from completing.

Files Attached:

The RTL design file rtl.v (the file where the issue occurs).

System Information:

Yosys version: [0.47+86]
Operating System: [Ubuntu 20.04]
[yosys_design.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/17930117/yosys_design.zip)

Could anyone please help me understand the cause of this parsing error? Any suggestions on how to resolve this issue would be greatly appreciated. Thank you for your assistance!

### Expected Behavior


Successfully parse and complete synthesis, and finally generate a netlist

### Actual Behavior

Parsing fail
